reproducibility_signals: Named tools (custom Postgres logical-replication plugin, WAL-shipping tuning, on-call runbook with named-escalation-owner) are real components of database-as-a-service operational architectures.
The active-incident scenario with day-six framing is a real pattern in DBaaS operations — replication-path incidents commonly run for days, not hours.
The pattern 'lead engineer with role-bound context gets a recruiter call mid-incident' is a documented engineering-org failure mode.
The implication offers reproducible alternative shapes (delayed start, transition runbook, paid contracting bridge) — these are well-established practices in the engineering-leaving literature.
verdict_reason: The analog reproduces in code (the custom WAL plugin is realistic), in organisational practice (the active-incident handover question), and in observable behaviour (the recruiter-call pattern). Not pure metaphor. PASS.
